question_structure.context: There are several identical small steel balls released from a certain position on an inclined plane every 0.1 seconds without initial velocity. After releasing several small steel balls continuously, a photograph is taken of the several small steel balls rolling on the inclined plane, as shown in the figure. It is measured that $AB = 15\mathrm{cm}$ and $BC = 20\mathrm{cm}$. The photograph has a 1:1 scale relationship with the actual object.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The velocity of ball B when taking the photo.
question_structure.sub_question_2: There are several small steel balls rolling on top of ball A.

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the principle that the average velocity equals the instantaneous velocity at the midpoint in time, we have $v_{B}={\frac{A B+B C}{2t}}=1.75\mathrm{{m/s}}$.

sub_question_2.step_2: According to $a={\frac{B C-A B}{t^{2}}}$, we obtain $a=5\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
sub_question_2.step_3: The falling time of ball $B$ is $t_{B}=\frac{\mathbf{v}_{B}}{a}=\frac{1.75}{5}\mathbf{s}=0.35\mathbf{s}$.
sub_question_2.step_4: The falling time of ball $A$ is $t_{A}=t_{B}-t=0.25\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_2.step_5: It can be determined that there are two small steel balls rolling above ball $A$.

image_captions: A line drawing depicts a series of four spheres rolling down an inclined plane, with the spheres labeled A, B and C.

step_1.physical_theorem: The average velocity equals the instantaneous velocity at the midpoint in time (for uniformly accelerated linear motion)

step_2.physical_theorem: Uniformly accelerated linear motion displacement formula

step_3.physical_theorem: Velocity-time relationship for uniformly accelerated linear motion

step_4.physical_theorem: Time interval between two adjacent balls

step_5.physical_theorem: Deduction from the time interval between two adjacent balls
